failedAssertions.reason: GET /onchain/networks/eth/pools returned meta.data_source="seeded" and only the three seeded Ethereum pools; reserve_usd/volume_usd.h24 values match fixture-style seeded responses rather than live-expanded catalog data.
GET /onchain/networks/eth/pools/0x88e6... returned meta.data_source="seeded" with fixture values and no live provider enrichment.
GET /onchain/networks/eth/pools/0x88e6.../ohlcv/hour returned attributes.source="fixture", so candles were not derived from live The Graph swap events on the running server.
GET /onchain/networks/eth/pools/0x88e6.../trades returned meta.source="fixture" with synthetic tx hashes, not live swap-event-backed trades.

GET /onchain/simple/networks/eth/token_price/... returned values, but they aligned with seeded aggregate fallbacks (e.g. market_cap_usd 680000000, total_reserve_in_usd 1005000000) and no direct live-path proof was observable on the running server.
GET /onchain/networks returned total_count 2, not more than 2 networks.
GET /onchain/networks/eth/dexes returned total_count 2, not more than 2 dexes.

frictions.description: The running validation server appeared to have no usable live onchain upstream data path: Ethereum pool/detail endpoints reported seeded data_source and trades/OHLCV reported fixture source despite upstream internet access existing for DeFiLlama protocols.

Solana pool detail/trade/OHLCV routes require normalized lowercase addresses; the canonical base58 address listed in /onchain/networks/solana/pools 404s when reused directly in detail routes because normalizeAddress lowercases it.
One targeted repository test intended to prove live catalog expansion currently fails in this repo because the provider spy is called 3 times instead of the asserted 1 time.

summary: Real API validation on http://127.0.0.1:3102 found 5 passing assertions (VAL-ONC-010/011/012/013/015) and 7 failing assertions. The running server consistently served seeded/fixture-backed Ethereum onchain responses instead of live-expanded DeFiLlama/The Graph data, while targeted repo tests still demonstrate intended degraded fallback behavior and OHLCV invariants; one live-expansion characterization test currently fails in-repo.
